solutions.solution:
I. It must be a climax
In order to be a valid punchline, a given statement must be the concluding part of a statement or a conversation. In other words, it should be the last part of a statement or conversation. The third key word is sudden twist. Whether they would form a complete conversation or story of these would qualify as a punchline only if it has an element of surprise.
To conclude, we can say that:
A given statement would fit as a punchline if it passes the following three tests:
I. It must be a climax.
II. It must be funny.
III. It must bring in sudden twist.
